result: DOUBLE DISSOCIATION (F-HOLO-1): deep holonomy net (RotorMeshNet) learns useful features THROUGH DEPTH, read by entropy not mean. 5 seeds held-out AUROC median: deep+entropy 0.759; deep+mean 0.542; shallow+entropy 0.561; shallow+mean 0.544. Remove EITHER depth OR entropy -> chance. Entirely closed-form / no autograd; end-to-end gradient FD-gated (max err 6e-5) BEFORE trusting training numbers.

novelty_classification.note: ON-MISSION deep-representation learning demonstrated (holonomy + entropy + closed-form, NOT the exact-solve devolution). Answers the empirical crux of 'one step': the representation is learned (bivectors), propagates through depth (closed-form adjoint/inverse-rotor transport), read by entropy.

components_added_or_updated: RotorMeshNet note: empirical half now DEMONSTRATED (was MECHANISM_DEMO mechanism-only)
guards_added: examples/holonomy_deep_dissociation.rs FD gate (end-to-end pipeline gradient) -- run before any training number is trusted

regression_tests_added: holonomy_net::deep_backward_matches_fd (mechanism, already present); example FD gate guards the full-pipeline gradient
integrity_note: Per evaluation-metric-integrity + never-run-blind: FD-gated the end-to-end closed-form gradient (a wrong gradient => phantom AUROC) BEFORE training; printed live loss; zero-mean task by construction so a raw mean is chance (no inflatable metric); AUROC as 5-seed median separability.
honest_scope: trained by GD on the closed-form gradient = stepping stone, NOT the instantaneous entropy-feedback thesis (entropy is the readout/objective, training is supervised BCE)
AUROC 0.759 above chance not saturated (hard task, gentle training)
environment_note: Mac (arm64, CPU); no kato15. kato15 still behind (Mac work since E7).

next_experiment_proposal.name: replace GD with the INSTANTANEOUS entropy/holonomy feedback (one-shot bivector update) = the last gap to the pure thesis; then push scale/score
